body.para: Qt Creator is the IDE used to develop and build EmulatR. It drives the CMake build, manages the compiler kit, and provides the editor and debugger; the project's reference build kit is Qt 6.10.2 with the MSVC 2022 64-bit toolchain.

EmulatR builds with CMake and a C++20 MSVC compiler. Qt Creator configures the CMake project against the selected kit and produces the usual Debug and RelWithDebInfo build trees. The emulator core is deliberately Qt-free -- Qt is used only by the console / UI subsystem -- so the dependency on Qt is confined to the front end even though the whole project is built through the Qt Creator environment.

Several behaviors are CMake options surfaced as build configuration rather than runtime flags. The most notable is EMULATR_DEBUG_STEP, which restores an -O0 build of the per-instruction step for single-step debugging; it is off by default so the hot loop ships optimized. Other compile-time guards (trace hooks, diagnostic channels) follow the same pattern and are described under CMake Macro Guards.
